heading: Application for payments; State plan
label: 300w–4 Application for payments; State plan
notes: Amendments1992— amended section generally, substituting present provisions for provisions relating to submission and form of application for assistance under this part as well as required assurances, public hearings on proposed use and distribution of funds, certifications by chief executive officer of State, and a description of intended use of funds as well as public access to and revision of such description.Pub. (d). inserted at end “The description shall include a statement of the public health objectives expected to be achieved by the State through the use of the payments the State will receive under .”Pub. L. 102–531, title I, § 103(b)106 Stat. 3473Oct. 27, 1992“With respect to compliance with the requirement established in subsection (c)(2) of section 1905 of the Public Health Service Act [] (as amended by subsection (a) of this section), a State is deemed, notwithstanding such section, to be in compliance with such requirement if the State establishes an advisory committee in accordance with subsection (d) of such section not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”42 U.S.C. 300w–4(c)(2)Oct. 27, 1992

children.text: the State submits to the Secretary an application for the payments;
the application contains a State plan in accordance with subsection (b);
the application contains the certification described in subsection (c);

the application contains such assurances as the Secretary may require regarding the compliance of the State with the requirements of this part (including assurances regarding compliance with the agreements described in subsection (c)); and
the application is in such form and is submitted by such date as the Secretary may require.
The plan is developed by the State agency with principal responsibility for public health programs, in consultation with the advisory committee established pursuant to subsection (c)(2).

The plan specifies the activities authorized in that are to be carried out with payments made to the State under , including a specification of the year 2000 health objectives for which the State will expend the payments.section 300w–3 of this titlesection 300w–2 of this title
The plan specifies the populations in the State for which such activities are to be carried out.
The plan specifies any populations in the State that have a disparate need for such activities.

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the programs and projects to be carried out;

children.label: B
children.text: an estimate of the number of individuals to be served by the programs and projects; and

children.label: C
children.text: an estimate of the number of public health personnel needed to carry out the strategy.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount to be expended for each population specified under paragraph (3); and

children.label: B
children.text: the amount to be expended for each population specified under paragraph (4).

children.label: i
children.text: the chief health officer of the State held public hearings on the plan; and

children.label: ii
children.text: proposals for the plan were made public in a manner that facilitated comments from public and private entities (including Federal and other public agencies).

children.label: B
children.text: The State agrees that, if any revisions are made in such plan during the fiscal year, the State will, with respect to the revisions, hold hearings and make proposals public in accordance with subparagraph (A), and will submit to the Secretary a description of the revisions.
The State has established an advisory committee in accordance with subsection (d).
The State agrees to expend payments under only for the activities authorized in .section 300w–2 of this titlesection 300w–3 of this title

The State agrees to expend such payments in accordance with the State plan submitted under subsection (a)(2) (with any revisions submitted to the Secretary under paragraph (1)(B)), including making expenditures to carry out the strategy contained in the plan pursuant to subsection (b)(5).

children.label: A
children.text: The State agrees that, in the case of each population for which such strategy is carried out, the State will measure the extent of progress being made toward improving the health status of the population.

children.label: i
children.text: the State will collect and report data in accordance with ; andsection 300w–5(a) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: for purposes of subparagraph (A), progress will be measured through use of each of the applicable uniform data items developed by the Secretary under paragraph (2) of such section, or if no such items are applicable, through use of the uniform criteria developed by the Secretary under paragraph (3) of such section.

With respect to the activities authorized in , the State agrees to maintain State expenditures for such activities at a level that is not less than the average level of such expenditures maintained by the State for the 2-year period preceding the fiscal year for which the State is applying to receive payments under .section 300w–3 of this titlesection 300w–2 of this title

The State agrees to establish reasonable criteria to evaluate the effective performance of entities that receive funds from such payments and procedures for procedural and substantive independent State review of the failure by the State to provide funds for any such entity.
The State agrees to permit and cooperate with Federal investigations undertaken in accordance with .section 300w–6 of this title

The State has in effect a system to protect from inappropriate disclosure patient and sex offense victim records maintained by the State in connection with an activity funded under this part or by any entity which is receiving payments from the allotment of the State under this part.

children.label: A
children.text: participate in the development of any plan by the State relating to emergency medical services, as such plan relates to highway safety; and

children.label: B
children.text: review and comment on any proposal by any State agency to use any Federal grant or Federal payment received by the State for the provision of emergency medical services as such proposal relates to highway safety.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of subsection (c)(2), an advisory committee is in accordance with this subsection if such committee is known as the State Preventive Health Advisory Committee (in this subsection referred to as the “Committee”) and the Committee meets the conditions described in the subsequent paragraphs of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: to hold public hearings on the State plan required in subsection (a)(2); and

children.label: i
children.text: the conduct of assessments of the public health;

children.label: ii
children.text: which of the activities authorized in should be carried out in the State;section 300w–3 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: the allocation of payments made to the State under ;section 300w–2 of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: the coordination of activities carried out under such plan with relevant programs of other entities; and

children.label: v
children.text: the collection and reporting of data in accordance with .section 300w–5(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: A condition under paragraph (1) for a State is that the Committee is composed of such members of the general public, and such officials of the health departments of political subdivisions of the State, as may be necessary to provide adequate representation of the general public and of such health departments.

children.label: B
children.text: With respect to compliance with subparagraph (A), the membership of advisory committees established pursuant to subsection (c)(2) may include representatives of community-based organizations (including minority community-based organizations), schools of public health, and entities to which the State involved awards grants or contracts to carry out activities authorized in .section 300w–3 of this title

children.heading: Chair; meetings
children.label: 4 Chair; meetings
children.text: A condition under paragraph (1) for a State is that the State public health officer serves as the chair of the Committee, and that the Committee meets not less than twice each fiscal year.
